title: Shallow seismic investigations of the accretionary complex offshore Central Chile
abstract: Thrust ridges are accretionary structures often associated with local uplift along splay faults and cold seep activity. The researchers examined the velocity structure of a thrust ridge system at the transition zone between the accretionary prism and continental shelf in central Chile using five seismic refraction lines with ocean bottom sensors. Results reveal a narrow margin-parallel volume (approximately 6 × 50 × 5 km³) whose velocity distribution is consistent with sedimentary rocks. The study found hydrate-bearing sediments within the Gas Hydrate Stability Zone showing minimal hydrate saturation. Fluid activity concentrated near thrust ridges correlates with elevated heat flow measurements and seepage patterns, proposing that splay faults facilitate tectonically-driven fluid migration vertically through the accretionary system.

location.description: Offshore central Chile between 35°S and 36°S, accretionary prism and continental shelf transition

data.name: Five OBS seismic refraction lines, central Chile accretionary prism
data.description: Five ocean-bottom seismometer seismic refraction lines acquired at the transition zone between the accretionary prism and continental shelf offshore central Chile (35°–36°S). Used for shallow velocity structure and gas hydrate estimation.

processing.summary: Five seismic refraction lines with ocean bottom sensors processed to derive P-wave velocity structure of the accretionary complex at 35°–36°S offshore central Chile. Travel time tomography applied to obtain 2D velocity cross-sections. Results reveal a ~6×50×5 km³ volume with sedimentary velocities consistent with thrust ridge structure. Gas hydrate concentration estimated from P-wave velocity anomalies within the Gas Hydrate Stability Zone.
workflow: 1. OBS deployment along five cross-margin refraction lines
5. Velocity anomaly interpretation for gas hydrate estimation
6. Integration with heat flow and seep data
processing.notes: Five refraction lines targeting accretionary prism thrust ridges. Bialas (GEOMAR) co-author suggests GEOMAR involvement in cruise.

analysis_notes: Paper behind paywall; no open access version found. Abstract reconstructed from Semantic Scholar summary and survey snippet. Seismic line coordinates are approximate (grid spacing not confirmed from paper). No public data repository identified.
